category_title: High-engagement primary students who value instructional support and a quiet environment
scenario_title: Distractibility during phonics drills and reading
minimal_context: The student is sitting in a comfy blue chair in the intervention room, working on building words with letter combinations before rereading a leveled text.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's start our phonics drill by building some words with these letters, and then we will reread our leveled text.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Okay. I like sitting in these comfy blue chairs. It’s really quiet in the intervention room.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Yes, it is. Go ahead and try building the first word.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Moves a few letters around) I don't know this one. I need getting help from the teacher so I don't have to wait a long time for help.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What do you think the next letter is? Just keep trying your best to figure it out.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Becomes somewhat less engaged and shows distractibility) I can't do it. (Stops looking at the letters and starts playing with the fabric on the chair instead).

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's start our phonics drill by building some words with these letters, and then we will reread our leveled text.

STUDENT: Okay. I like sitting in these comfy blue chairs. It’s really quiet in the intervention room.

TUTOR: Yes, it is. Go ahead and try building the first word.

STUDENT: (Moves a few letters around) I don't know this one. I need getting help from the teacher so I don't have to wait a long time for help.

TUTOR: What do you think the next letter is? Just keep trying your best to figure it out.

STUDENT: (Becomes somewhat less engaged and shows distractibility) I can't do it. (Stops looking at the letters and starts playing with the fabric on the chair instead).

task.content: do various Wilson Fundations drills including reviewing sight words, reviewing letters/letter combinations in conjunction with associated sounds and key words, building words, sorting words, and writing words, and reread one or more LLI-leveled texts

context.environment: quieter room with comfy blue chairs within the intervention setting as compared to the classroom
context.materials: LLI-leveled texts, Wilson Fundations drills, stickers
context.social_setting: getting help from the teacher, (making and) being with friends

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: During videotaped intervention sessions involving reading and phonics tasks
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: exhibits distractibility and is somewhat less engaged
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: appreciates the quiet room because it enabled her to concentrate on completing her reading
related_quotes: It’s really quiet [in the intervention room]
